# DBMLX Language — AI Reference

Version: 0.1.9

DBMLX (`.dbmlx`) is a superset of DBML. All standard DBML syntax is valid.
This file documents only the dbmlx-specific extensions that don't exist in standard DBML.

---

## !include — multi-file schemas

Split large schemas across files. Paths are relative to the including file.
Includes are resolved before parsing; the parser sees one stitched document.

```dbmlx
!include "auth/users.dbmlx"
!include "billing/invoices.dbmlx"

Ref: users.id < invoices.user_id
```

---

## DiagramView — named filtered views

Define multiple named views of the same schema. Each view has its own layout file.
Views filter which tables are shown; the underlying schema is unchanged.

```dbmlx
DiagramView auth_context {
  Tables { users, sessions, roles }
}

DiagramView billing_overview {
  TableGroups { billing }
}

DiagramView tenant_schema {
  Schemas { tenant }
}

DiagramView everything {
  Tables { * }
}
```

Sections inside a view:
- `Tables { name, name, ... }` — show only these tables (use `*` for all)
- `TableGroups { name, ... }` — show all tables belonging to these TableGroups
- `Schemas { name, ... }` — show all tables in these schemas

Multiple sections in one view are unioned.

---

## Migration diff annotations

Annotate columns to visualize a schema migration as a before/after diff in the diagram.

### Key rule: write the column in its NEW (post-migration) state.
Record the original values inside the annotation. This means Refs and indexes
always reference the new column name, which is correct for the post-migration schema.

```dbmlx
Table orders {
  id           int           [pk]
  status       varchar(50)

  amount       decimal(10,2) [add]
  // new column being added — rendered with green accent

  total        decimal       [drop]
  // column being removed — rendered with red strikethrough

  customer_id  int           [modify: name="customer", type="varchar(100)"]
  // renamed + retyped: NEW name/type on the line, ORIGINAL name/type in [modify:]
  // rendered as two rows: original (strikethrough) → new (amber)

  email        varchar(255)  [modify: type="varchar(100)"]
  // type change only — omit name= when the column name is unchanged

  notes        text          [not null, drop]
  // [drop] and [add] combine freely with standard column settings
}

// Refs use the NEW column name
Ref: orders.customer_id > customers.id
```

Annotation summary:
- `[add]`  — column is being added; does not exist before migration
- `[drop]` — column is being removed; will not exist after migration
- `[modify: name="old_name" type="old_type"]` — column renamed and/or retyped;
  both `name=` and `type=` are optional (omit whichever didn't change)

---

## Standard DBML — quick reference

```dbmlx
Table schema.table_name [headercolor: "#hex"] {
  id           int           [pk, increment]
  name         varchar(255)  [not null, unique]
  status       job_status
  created_at   timestamp     [default: `now()`]
  meta         jsonb         [null, note: 'extra data']
  user_id      int           [ref: > users.id]

  indexes {
    (name, status) [name: "idx_name_status", unique]
  }

  Note: 'Free-form table description'
}

Enum job_status {
  created
  running
  done
  failure [note: 'terminal state']
}

TableGroup billing {
  orders
  invoices
  payments
}

// Refs (can be top-level or inline)
Ref: orders.user_id > users.id     // many-to-one
Ref: users.id < orders.user_id     // one-to-many
Ref: users.id - profiles.user_id   // one-to-one
Ref: tags.id <> posts.id           // many-to-many

// Composite FK
Ref: orders.(user_id, tenant_id) > users.(id, tenant_id)
```
